title: Stockton South

infobox.value: * [County Durham](/wiki/County_Durham "County Durham") * [North Yorkshire](/wiki/North_Yorkshire "North Yorkshire") * (*[Tees Valley](/wiki/Tees_Valley "Tees Valley") combined authority*)

[Stockton-on-Tees](/wiki/Stockton-on-Tees_(UK_Parliament_constituency) "Stockton-on-Tees (UK Parliament constituency)"), [Thornaby](/wiki/Thornaby_(UK_Parliament_constituency) "Thornaby (UK Parliament constituency)"), [Easington](/wiki/Easington_(UK_Parliament_constituency) "Easington (UK Parliament constituency)") and [Richmond (Yorks)](/wiki/Richmond_(Yorks) "Richmond (Yorks)")[[2]](#cite_note-ElectionWeb83-2)

was a [constituency](/wiki/List_of_United_Kingdom_Parliament_constituencies "List of United Kingdom Parliament constituencies")[[n 1]](#cite_note-3) represented in the [House of Commons](/wiki/House_of_Commons_of_the_United_Kingdom "House of Commons of the United Kingdom") of the [UK Parliament](/wiki/UK_Parliament "UK Parliament") from 2019 until its abolition for the [2024 general election](/wiki/2024_United_Kingdom_general_election "2024 United Kingdom general election") by [Matt Vickers](/wiki/Matt_Vickers "Matt Vickers") of the [Conservative Party](/wiki/Conservative_Party_(UK) "Conservative Party (UK)").[[n 2]](#cite_note-4)

The Borough of Stockton-on-Tees wards of Bishopsgarth, Egglescliffe, Fairfield, Grangefield, Hartburn, Ingleby Barwick, Mandale, Parkfield, Preston, Stainsby, Victoria, Village, and Yarm, and the Borough of Middlesbrough wards of Ayresome, Brookfield, and Kader.

The Borough of Stockton-on-Tees wards of Bishopsgarth, Egglescliffe, Elm Tree, Fairfield, Grangefield, Hartburn, Ingleby Barwick, Mandale, Parkfield, Preston, Stainsby, Victoria, Village, and Yarm. *The three Middlesbrough wards were transferred to the redrawn Middlesbrough constituency.*

The Borough of Stockton-on-Tees wards of Bishopsgarth and Elm Tree, Eaglescliffe, Fairfield, Grangefield, Hartburn, Ingleby Barwick East, Ingleby Barwick West, Mandale and Victoria, Parkfield and Oxbridge, Stainsby Hill, Thornaby-on-Tees, and Yarm.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Parliamentary constituency in the United Kingdom, 1983–2024

| Stockton South | |
| --- | --- |
| Former [borough constituency](/wiki/Borough_constituency "Borough constituency") for the [House of Commons](/wiki/House_of_Commons_of_the_United_Kingdom "House of Commons of the United Kingdom") | |
| [Outline map](/wiki/File:StocktonSouth2007Constituency.svg) 2010–2024 boundary of Stockton South in the former county of [Cleveland](/wiki/Cleveland_(county) "Cleveland (county)") | |
| [Outline map](/wiki/File:EnglandCleveland.svg) Location of the former county of [Cleveland](/wiki/Cleveland_(county) "Cleveland (county)") within England | |
| [County](/wiki/Ceremonial_counties_of_England "Ceremonial counties of England") | * [County Durham](/wiki/County_Durham "County Durham") * [North Yorkshire](/wiki/North_Yorkshire "North Yorkshire") * (*[Tees Valley](/wiki/Tees_Valley "Tees Valley") combined authority*) |
| [Electorate](/wiki/Electoral_registration_in_the_United_Kingdom "Electoral registration in the United Kingdom") | 74,698 (2018)[[1]](#cite_note-1) |
| Major settlements | [Stockton](/wiki/Stockton-on-Tees "Stockton-on-Tees"), [Thornaby](/wiki/Thornaby "Thornaby"), [Yarm](/wiki/Yarm "Yarm"), [Ingleby Barwick](/wiki/Ingleby_Barwick "Ingleby Barwick") and [Eaglescliffe](/wiki/Eaglescliffe "Eaglescliffe") |
| [1983](/wiki/1983_United_Kingdom_general_election "1983 United Kingdom general election")–[2024](/wiki/2024_United_Kingdom_general_election "2024 United Kingdom general election") | |
| Seats | One |
| Created from | [Stockton-on-Tees](/wiki/Stockton-on-Tees_(UK_Parliament_constituency) "Stockton-on-Tees (UK Parliament constituency)"), [Thornaby](/wiki/Thornaby_(UK_Parliament_constituency) "Thornaby (UK Parliament constituency)"), [Easington](/wiki/Easington_(UK_Parliament_constituency) "Easington (UK Parliament constituency)") and [Richmond (Yorks)](/wiki/Richmond_(Yorks) "Richmond (Yorks)")[[2]](#cite_note-ElectionWeb83-2) |
| Replaced by | [Stockton West](/wiki/Stockton_West "Stockton West") |

**Stockton South** was a [constituency](/wiki/List_of_United_Kingdom_Parliament_constituencies "List of United Kingdom Parliament constituencies")[[n 1]](#cite_note-3) represented in the [House of Commons](/wiki/House_of_Commons_of_the_United_Kingdom "House of Commons of the United Kingdom") of the [UK Parliament](/wiki/UK_Parliament "UK Parliament") from 2019 until its abolition for the [2024 general election](/wiki/2024_United_Kingdom_general_election "2024 United Kingdom general election") by [Matt Vickers](/wiki/Matt_Vickers "Matt Vickers") of the [Conservative Party](/wiki/Conservative_Party_(UK) "Conservative Party (UK)").[[n 2]](#cite_note-4)

Under the [2023 review of Westminster constituencies](/wiki/2023_review_of_Westminster_constituencies "2023 review of Westminster constituencies"), the seat was abolished; subject to moderate boundary changes, it was reformed as **[Stockton West](/wiki/Stockton_West "Stockton West")**.[[3]](#cite_note-5)

## Boundaries

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Stockton_South&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Boundaries")]

![Map](https://maps.wikimedia.org/img/osm-intl,a,a,a,300x200.png?lang=en&domain=en.wikipedia.org&title=Stockton_South&revid=1344780590&groups=_e1a31fa5eea4b02f8d48c926450efca1fdc04084&parser=legacy)

Map of 2010–2024 boundaries

**1983–1997**: The Borough of Stockton-on-Tees wards of Bishopsgarth, Egglescliffe, Fairfield, Grangefield, Hartburn, Ingleby Barwick, Mandale, Parkfield, Preston, Stainsby, Victoria, Village, and Yarm, and the Borough of Middlesbrough wards of Ayresome, Brookfield, and Kader.

**1997–2010**: The Borough of Stockton-on-Tees wards of Bishopsgarth, Egglescliffe, Elm Tree, Fairfield, Grangefield, Hartburn, Ingleby Barwick, Mandale, Parkfield, Preston, Stainsby, Victoria, Village, and Yarm. *The three Middlesbrough wards were transferred to the redrawn Middlesbrough constituency.*

**2010–2024**: The Borough of Stockton-on-Tees wards of Bishopsgarth and Elm Tree, Eaglescliffe, Fairfield, Grangefield, Hartburn, Ingleby Barwick East, Ingleby Barwick West, Mandale and Victoria, Parkfield and Oxbridge, Stainsby Hill, Thornaby-on-Tees, and Yarm.

*2024: Constituency was abolished as a result of [2023 review of Westminster constituencies](/wiki/2023_review_of_Westminster_constituencies "2023 review of Westminster constituencies").*

Stockton South consisted of the south-western half of [Stockton-on-Tees](/wiki/Stockton-on-Tees "Stockton-on-Tees") and on the same bank, upstream, the town of [Eaglescliffe](/wiki/Eaglescliffe "Eaglescliffe") – on the southern bank of the [River Tees](/wiki/River_Tees "River Tees") are the towns of [Thornaby-on-Tees](/wiki/Thornaby-on-Tees "Thornaby-on-Tees"), [Yarm](/wiki/Yarm "Yarm"), and [Ingleby Barwick](/wiki/Ingleby_Barwick "Ingleby Barwick").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Stockton_South&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: History")]

The seat was formed from a combination of [Stockton-on-Tees](/wiki/Stockton-on-Tees_(UK_Parliament_constituency) "Stockton-on-Tees (UK Parliament constituency)") and [Thornaby](/wiki/Thornaby_(UK_Parliament_constituency) "Thornaby (UK Parliament constituency)") in 1983, predominantly as a replacement to the latter seat.

Political history

More middle-class than neighbouring [Stockton North](/wiki/Stockton_North "Stockton North"), this seat was first won by the [SDP–Liberal Alliance](/wiki/SDP%E2%80%93Liberal_Alliance "SDP–Liberal Alliance") in a narrow victory at the [1983](/wiki/1983_United_Kingdom_general_election "1983 United Kingdom general election"). Ian Wrigglesworth, the former [Labour](/wiki/Labour_Party_(UK) "Labour Party (UK)") MP for Thornaby, defected to the newly formed [Social Democratic Party](/wiki/Social_Democratic_Party_(UK) "Social Democratic Party (UK)") in 1981, and held the successor seat as the SDP candidate.

This result came after the Conservative candidate's nomination was withdrawn when he was revealed to have previously been in the [National Front](/wiki/National_Front_(UK) "National Front (UK)").

Following this, the seat was held by a Conservative for ten years, from [1987](/wiki/1987_United_Kingdom_general_election "1987 United Kingdom general election") to 1997. It was a [bellwether](/wiki/Bellwether_(politics) "Bellwether (politics)") in Labour's landslide at the [1997 general election](/wiki/1997_United_Kingdom_general_election "1997 United Kingdom general election"), and its member, [Dari Taylor](/wiki/Dari_Taylor "Dari Taylor"), retained it until the [2010 general election](/wiki/2010_United_Kingdom_general_election "2010 United Kingdom general election"), when the Conservative, [James Wharton](/wiki/James_Wharton,_Baron_Wharton_of_Yarm "James Wharton, Baron Wharton of Yarm") narrowly won back the seat.

It was the [Conservative Party](/wiki/Conservative_Party_(UK) "Conservative Party (UK)")'s only gain in the North East, with Wharton substantially increasing his majority at the [2015 general election](/wiki/2015_United_Kingdom_general_election "2015 United Kingdom general election"). However, Labour's victory in the seat in [2017](/wiki/2017_United_Kingdom_general_election "2017 United Kingdom general election") saw the seat's 30 year status as a bellwether constituency come to an end. In 2019, the Conservatives took it back, in line with the general swing in their favour in multiple [north east](/wiki/North_East_England "North East England") [red wall](/wiki/Red_wall_(British_politics) "Red wall (British politics)") seats, despite only being held by Labour for fifteen of its 39 years of existence.

## Constituency profile

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Stockton_South&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Constituency profile")]

Based on ONS data, workless claimants and registered jobseekers, were in May 2017 lower than the North East average of 5.9%[[4]](#cite_note-6) and also lower than the national average of 4.6%, at 3.4%[[5]](#cite_note-7) of the population.

|  | [1987](/wiki/1987_United_Kingdom_general_election "1987 United Kingdom general election") | [Tim Devlin](/wiki/Tim_Devlin "Tim Devlin") | [Conservative](/wiki/Conservative_Party_(UK) "Conservative Party (UK)") |
|  | [1997](/wiki/1997_United_Kingdom_general_election "1997 United Kingdom general election") | [Dari Taylor](/wiki/Dari_Taylor "Dari Taylor") | [Labour](/wiki/Labour_Party_(UK) "Labour Party (UK)") |
|  | [2010](/wiki/2010_United_Kingdom_general_election "2010 United Kingdom general election") | [James Wharton](/wiki/James_Wharton,_Baron_Wharton_of_Yarm "James Wharton, Baron Wharton of Yarm") | [Conservative](/wiki/Conservative_Party_(UK) "Conservative Party (UK)") |

## Notes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Stockton_South&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Notes")]

1. **[^](#cite_ref-3)** A [borough constituency](/wiki/Borough_constituency "Borough constituency") (for the purposes of election expenses and type of returning officer)
2. **[^](#cite_ref-4)** As with all constituencies, the constituency elects one [Member of Parliament](/wiki/Member_of_Parliament_(United_Kingdom) "Member of Parliament (United Kingdom)") (MP) by the [first past the post](/wiki/First_past_the_post "First past the post") system of election at least every five years.
